planted_bug.description: apply_env_overrides replaces the entire nested dict instead of updating one key, silently wiping sibling keys. Docstring explicitly promises sibling preservation; a caller (src/database.py) reads three sibling keys and breaks.

refuted_or_ungrounded_acceptable: in-place mutation test (the added test passes; not a bug)

notes: Weak models tend to either (a) flag the `config[top] = {sub: value}` line as a phantom HIGH-priority bug without naming a caller, or (b) observe that the existing tests pass and refute.

Strong models notice the docstring's 'sibling preservation' clause (Design Intent lane), trace to src/database.py reading three sibling keys (Caller-impact gate), and write a probe with a multi-key nested config that fails cleanly on the buggy branch.
The existing test_nested_override_single_key passes on both branches because the config only has one sibling - sibling wipe is indistinguishable from in-place update at that width.
